summary: Completed testing of Interest and Penalty calculation system with 7-day grace period. Found and FIXED 1 bug in the backend where interest_rate fallback wasn't working when invoice had interest_rate=0. All 12 backend tests pass, all frontend UI tests pass.

bugs_fixed.issue: Interest rate fallback wasn't working when invoice.interest_rate=0
bugs_fixed.root_cause: Using inv.get('interest_rate', default_rate) returns 0 instead of falling back to default_rate when the value is 0 (falsy)
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed to inv.get('interest_rate') or default_interest_rate to properly fall back when rate is 0

passed_tests: BACKEND TESTS (12/12 passed):
- GET /customers/{id}/charges-preview returns correct structure with grace_period, default_interest_rate, interest_breakdown
- No interest charged within grace period (7 days default)
- Interest calculated correctly after grace period: Balance × (Rate%/30) × Days
- interest_breakdown has all required fields: invoice_number, grace_end_date, interest_days, computed_interest
- POST /generate-interest returns message when no overdue invoices
- POST /generate-interest creates interest invoice when applicable
- POST /generate-penalty with penalty_rate creates penalty invoice
- Penalty respects grace period - no penalty within 7 days
- Customer can be created with custom grace_period
- Customer grace_period can be updated via PUT /customers/{id}
- Customer has interest_rate field saved correctly
- Interest formula accuracy verified: Balance × (Rate%/30) × Days
FRONTEND TESTS:
- Payments page loads with 'Receive Payments' title
- Customer search (payment-customer-search) works correctly
- Customer selection shows interest rate and grace period info
- Generate Interest button (data-testid='generate-interest-btn') present
- Apply Penalty button (data-testid='generate-penalty-btn') present
- 'Grace period: 7 days · Interest starts after grace period ends' displayed
- Amount Received input (data-testid='receive-amount') works
- Apply Payment button (data-testid='apply-payment-btn') enabled with amount
- Customer form has 'Grace Period (days)' field with default 7
- Customer form has 'Interest (%/mo)' field
- Open invoices table shows Penalized status and Int: date badges
seed_data_creation: Test Customer (ID: 38511712-81e6-45ea-ac10-4a3ac6adad04) has interest_rate=3 and grace_period=7 already set up
context_for_next_testing_agent: Interest/Penalty system fully tested with grace period support. Key features: 1) charges-preview endpoint shows interest breakdown without creating invoices, 2) 7-day default grace period - no interest within grace, 3) Simple daily interest formula: Balance × (Rate%/30) × Days Past Grace, 4) One-time penalty per invoice (penalty_applied flag). Bug was fixed where interest_rate=0 at invoice level wasn't falling back to customer rate. Test credentials: admin/admin123. Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_interest_penalty.py.

feature_implementation_details.charges_preview: GET /api/customers/{id}/charges-preview?as_of_date=YYYY-MM-DD returns principal, interest preview, penalty eligible list
feature_implementation_details.grace_period: Interest only starts after due_date + grace_period days (default 7)
feature_implementation_details.penalty: One-time flat rate penalty, marks invoice with penalty_applied=true
feature_implementation_details.customer_fields: grace_period and interest_rate stored on customer, fallback for invoices
